ID-0b_specific.2AAI: a plant-derived N-glycosylase that depurinates a specific adenine in 28S ribosomal RNA, irreversibly inactivating the ribosome
ID-0b_specific.3BTA: a bacterial zinc metalloprotease that cleaves SNARE protein SNAP-25 at a single peptide bond, blocking neurotransmitter release
ID-0b_specific.1ACC: a bacterial heptameric pore-forming protein that binds host cell receptors and translocates enzymatic components across the membrane
ID-0b_specific.1A2P: a small bacterial ribonuclease (110 residues) that cleaves single-stranded RNA with no known clinical significance

ID-0b_generic.2AAI: a type II ribosome-inactivating protein with an enzymatic A-chain and a lectin B-chain
ID-0b_generic.3BTA: a di-chain neurotoxin with a zinc-dependent endopeptidase light chain and a receptor-binding heavy chain
ID-0b_generic.1ACC: a component of a binary system that forms a heptameric prepore on host cell membranes
ID-0b_generic.1A2P: a small single-domain enzyme used as a model system in protein folding studies
